description: This figure presents a schematic illustration of the complete thermo-mechanical processing route for two low-density medium-Mn steels, M1 (Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C) and M2 (Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C), accompanied by SEM micrographs captured after each processing stage. The temperature-time profile shows: as-cast samples subjected to homogenization at 1100°C followed by water quenching, subsequent hot rolling at 1100°C with air cooling, and final intercritical annealing at 700°C for 1 h followed by water quenching. Corresponding micrographs display the microstructural evolution: M1-AC/M2-AC (as-cast), M1-HQ/M2-HQ (homogenized and quenched), M1-HR/M2-HR (hot-rolled and air-cooled), and M1-IA/M2-IA (intercritically annealed), with scale bars ranging from 3 μm to 500 μm. The images reveal distinct phase morphologies at each step, with M2 exhibiting δ-ferrite formation due to Si addition, while M1 develops a finer dual-phase ferrite-austenite structure after intercritical annealing. This figure establishes the processing-microstructure relationship, demonstrating that Si-induced δ-ferrite significantly alters austenite fraction and stability during intercritical annealing, thereby governing the final mechanical performance.
